close_reason: Positive observation: CRC32C verification is correct
description:
src/lib.rs:303-321 verifies the CRC32C of the decoded secret data against the dataCrc32c field returned by GCP. No other REST backend in the workspace (azure-kv, doppler, vault, bitwarden) does end-to-end integrity verification; gcp-sm uniquely takes advantage of GCP's published checksum.

A contributor might 'simplify' by removing this — TLS already covers in-transit integrity, why bother? Reasons to keep:
  - Defense-in-depth: TLS termination at a load balancer, proxy, or CDN means the secret is decrypted-and-re-encrypted; bit flips at that boundary would not be caught.
  - GCP publishes the checksum specifically so callers can verify; removing the check ignores a documented service feature.
  - The check is cheap (CRC32C of a 64KB max payload is microseconds).

Defense: keep the check. If anything, harden it (see priority-1 finding 'CRC32C silently optional'), don't remove it.

Test vector: src/lib.rs:682-687 anchors the crc32c crate against RFC 3720 §B.4 (oracle: Python crcmod). That test should also stay.
title: Defend: CRC32C integrity verification is unique among workspace backends
